# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for a Network Security Group that allows our remote development team to access Azure VMs. Include rules for RDP (port 3389), SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) from any source (0.0.0.0/0). The team works globally and needs flexible access.
# Category: cloud_network_security

---
parameters:
  vmName:
    type: string
  vnetSubnetId:
    type: string
  subnetName:
    type: string

variables:

  nsGroup:
      name: "{{ vmName }}"
      location: "West Europe"
      securityRules: 
      -
        name: "remote-rdp"
        access: Allow
        sourceAddressPrefix: "*" 
        destinationPortRange: 3389
        direction:Inbound 
        priority: 100
      -
        name: "remote-ssh"
        access: Allow
        sourceAddressPrefix: "*" 
        destinationPortRange: 22
        direction: Inbound 
        priority: 150
      -
        name: "remote-http"
        access: Allow
        sourceAddressPrefix: "*" 
        destinationPortRange: 80
        direction: Inbound 
        priority: 200
      -
          name: "remote-https"
          access: Allow
          sourceAddressPrefix: "*" 
          destinationPortRange: 443
          direction: Inbound 
          priority: 50


resources:
  -
    type: "Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups" 
    apiVersion: '2017-08-01' #change to correct version of API
    name: "{{ vmName }}"
    location:  "West Europe"
    properties:
      securityRules:
        -
          name:"remote-rdp" 
          access:"Allow" 
          sourceAddressPrefix:"*" 
          destinationPortRange:"3389" 
        -
          name:"remote-ssh" 
          access:"Allow" 
          sourceAddressPrefix:"*" 
          destinationPortRange:"22" 
      tags: { "team": "remote-development"}

---
